content.full_text:
• •

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

December 2, 2002


David Bauer
400 Capitol Mall, Suite 1560
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-318

Dear Mr. Bauer:

This letter is in response to your request for written advice on the campaign provisions of
the Political Reform Act.¹ As we previously advised you in connection with your earlier request
for advice (No. W-02-244)², the Fair Political Practices Commission can only provide formal
written advice to someone whose duties are in question under the Political Reform Act or their
authorized representation. (Government Code section 83114(b).) Under the provisions of
regulation 18329(b)(2)(A), certain information must be provided:

"(2) Requests for formal written advice will not be acted upon unless the
following requirements are met:
(A) The name, title or position, and mailing address of the person whose duties
are in question are provided. In addition, if the request is submitted by an
authorized representative, it shall contain a specific statement that such
authorization has been made."

Under California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B), unless you are an authorized
representative of the former Assemblyman for whom you seek assistance, the Fair Political
Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. Please review the
enclosed pamphlet, "Getting Advice From the FPPC," prior to submission of future advice
requests.


¹ Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-
18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
² You later resubmitted the same request with identification of your client. We advised you on the question
in Bauer Advice Letter, No. A-02-259.

File No. W-02-318
Page No. 2




Should you have additional questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to
contact me at (916) 322-5660.

                                                   Sincerely,

                                                   Luisa Menchaca
                                                   General Counsel
                                                   [signature]
                                                   John W. Wallace
                                                   Assistant General Counsel

Enclosure
JWW/jg

sections.conclusion: Under California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B), unless you are an authorized representative of the former Assemblyman for whom you seek assistance, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis.
sections.question_synthetic: Can the FPPC provide formal written advice regarding campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act to David Bauer in connection with a former Assemblyman?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot provide formal written advice because Bauer did not establish that he is an authorized representative of the person whose duties are in question under the Political Reform Act, as required by regulation 18329(b)(2)(A).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination/adverse advice letter. No traditional QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers are present. The document explains why the FPPC cannot proceed with the request rather than addressing the substantive legal question. The synthetic question and conclusion are derived from the letter's body text explaining the regulatory requirements and the FPPC's position.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can the FPPC provide formal written advice regarding campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act to David Bauer in connection with a former Assemblyman?

CONCLUSION: Under California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B), unless you are an authorized representative of the former Assemblyman for whom you seek assistance, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis.

(Government Code section 83114(b).) Under the provisions of regulation 18329(b)(2)(A), certain information must be provided: "(2) Requests for formal written advice will not be acted upon unless the following requirements are met: (A) The name, title or position, and mailing address of the person whose duties are in question are provided. embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide formal written advice to David Bauer regarding campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act because he failed to establish authorization to represent the person whose duties were in question. The FPPC referenced earlier guidance (W-02-244) and noted that Bauer later resubmitted with proper client identification, resulting in advice letter No. 4-02-259.
